status_notes: Initial research from Google Cloud KMS documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs) and partial Pass 2 (API). Needs Pass 3 (cross-reference) and Pass 5 (human review with live GCP environment).
notes: Cloud key management with software, HSM, and external key options. Integrates with 30+ Google Cloud services for encryption at rest.

data.description: Keys generated and used within Cloud KMS software. FIPS 140-2 Level 1. Lowest cost option.

options.name: Symmetric AES-256-GCM
options.description: 256-bit AES-GCM symmetric encryption key. Most common.

options.name: EC P-256
options.description: Elliptic curve P-256 key for signing

options.name: EC P-384
options.description: Elliptic curve P-384 key for signing

data.description: Keys generated and used within FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated HSMs. Multi-tenant by default. Single-tenant option available for dedicated HSM partitions with quorum-based admin.

options.name: Multi-tenant HSM
options.description: Shared HSM pool. Default. Lower cost.

configure.terraform: google_kms_crypto_key with purpose = ENCRYPT_DECRYPT and version_template { protection_level = HSM }

data.description: Keys held in your own external key manager, never sent to Google. Connected over internet or VPC. Integrates with 30+ Google Cloud services. Requires compatible third-party EKM.

options.name: EKM over internet
options.description: External key manager connected over public internet.

options.name: EKM over VPC
options.description: External key manager connected over VPC network. Higher security.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use EKM over VPC for enhanced security. Coordinate key rotation with your EKM provider.

audit.cli: gcloud kms keys list --keyring={ring} --location={loc} (check protectionLevel=EXTERNAL or EXTERNAL_VPC)

configure.terraform: google_kms_crypto_key with purpose = ENCRYPT_DECRYPT and version_template { protection_level = EXTERNAL }

data.description: Automatic rotation for symmetric keys on a configurable schedule. New key version becomes primary for encryption; old versions kept for decryption. Asymmetric and external keys require manual rotation.

default.note: Must configure rotation period. No automatic rotation by default.

audit.cli: gcloud kms keys describe {key} --keyring={ring} --location={loc} (check rotationPeriod, nextRotationTime)

data.description: Each key has one or more versions. One version is primary (used for new encryptions). Versions can be enabled, disabled, or destroyed independently. Destroyed versions cannot decrypt existing data (crypto-shredding).
Import externally generated key material into Cloud KMS. Supports manual wrapping (OpenSSL) and automatic wrapping. Post-import verification available.
Automatically provisions key rings, keys, and service accounts during resource creation. Eliminates manual pre-provisioning. Supports both software and HSM protection levels.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Autokey for consistent key management without manual provisioning overhead.

data.description: Pattern where Cloud KMS wraps a data encryption key (DEK) with a key encryption key (KEK). DEK encrypts data locally; KEK-encrypted DEK stored alongside data. Standard pattern for all Google Cloud services using CMEK.
